age_range.note: 'N+' compact notation.
Birth-year phrasing — implies max age = current_year - birth_year. As of 2026: born after 1985 -> max_age 41. Convention call: corpus uses 2026 reference.
Inverse birth-year: born before 1980 -> min_age 46 (2026 ref).
Generic age-context fallback should catch the 40 and 45 numbers; 'around' modifier may make it broader.

age_range.note: Compound prompt — sea_service + age. Legacy 'over N' catches age side; sea_service is separate unapplied family.

age_range.note: 'aged N to N years' — range_patterns should catch via 'aged' anchor.

controls.note: Figurative 'without passport restrictions' — does not assert passport requirement.
Polarity inversion: opposite of must_be_valid intent.
Wrong sense — 'passport' refers to photo format, not the document. Legacy currently fires passport_validity; shadow/active normalizer must require document-validity context.
Figurative age expression — must NOT fire age_range. _AGE_FIGURATIVE_PATTERNS covers 'middle-aged'.
'Senior officer' is a rank descriptor, not a numeric age constraint. Should not fire age_range.
Generic 'safety training' without STCW/BST/basic cues — must not fire stcw_basic. _STCW_BASIC_CONTEXT_CUES requires explicit anchor.
'MV' as vessel prefix, NOT a Maritime Crew Visa reference. Should not fire us_visa with visa_group=australia.
Qualitative recruiter prompt; should map to semantic_query only, no hard-filter family.
controls.prompt: good candidate with stable sea service

controls.note: Two figurative age expressions ('senior captain', 'youthful') — must not fire age_range.
controls.prompt: experienced senior captain who is youthful in approach

controls.note: Generic 'certificate' without specific named certificate — must not fire certificate_requirement with empty or invented list.
Anti-constraint on rank — must not fire rank_match. Convention: ignore figurative rank phrasings.
controls.prompt: candidate must be flexible about rank

controls.note: Bare 'documents' without 'passport' keyword — must not fall through legacy passport fallback. Tests the anchor strictness.

passport_validity.note: Number-first word order with 'remaining'; legacy window_patterns require 'passport valid(ity)' adjacency.
'months left' phrasing absent from legacy patterns.
Legacy catches boolean passport validity but drops the year-unit minimum. Convention: 1 year -> 12 months.
passport_validity.prompt: passport valid for at least 1 year

passport_validity.note: Legacy catches boolean passport validity but drops the year-unit minimum. Convention: 2 years -> 24 months.
'Expiring within X months' is an inverse phrasing the LLM should interpret as 'must have X+ months remaining'. Convention call: human review the boundary.
passport_validity.prompt: passport expiring within 3 months should be rejected

passport_validity.note: Renewed/fresh implies valid; no explicit 'valid' keyword.
Newly issued implies valid; no explicit 'valid' keyword.
Negation of expiry == validity intent. Distinguish from polarity-inversion controls like 'expired passport OK'.
Legacy fallback may fire due to 'current' context word but with no 'passport ... valid' adjacency the requested_label may be wrong. Manually verify.
Legacy may catch boolean validity but not the months value due to 'minimum' interspersed with 'validity remaining' phrasing.
passport_validity.prompt: passport must have minimum 6 months validity remaining

passport_validity.note: Conditional expiry phrasing. Convention: 'in the next year' -> 12 months minimum remaining.
passport_validity.prompt: candidate should hold a passport that doesn't expire in the next year

passport_validity.note: Date-based expiry. Legacy fallback may fire from 'valid' context but cannot compute months remaining (depends on evaluation date). Convention: drop minimum_months_remaining for date-based prompts and surface as date-based hard filter when supported.
